name: Danny Kaye
description: American actor, singer, dancer, and comedian (1911–1987)
abstract: Danny Kaye (born David Daniel Kaminsky; January 18, 1911 – March 3, 1987) was an American actor, comedian, singer, and dancer. His performances featured physical comedy, idiosyncratic pantomimes, and rapid-fire novelty songs.

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Simmons
about_quotes.evidence: She brought in a record of Danny Kaye. And the same thing she wanted them to understand what the voice could really do.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norman Simmons interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 30, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: Louis Armstrong, Benny Goodman, [Walter] Sid Catlett Danny Kaye and the Lane Sisters... Did you dance in that film? ... Danny Kaye. And he had a girl ... less singing from Buck and Bubbles

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Because Zutty worked in them, he didn't know what they were called. And then now about Danny Kaye's picture. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Armstrong
about_quotes.evidence: Yes, Danny Kay and all of them. Just during the time we made five pennants... And Dick Powell was cute when we made Going Places

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: I was used to hearing scat singing from the older days, Cab Calloway, some of Louis Armstrong’s things, Danny Kaye [scats].
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Katz
about_quotes.evidence: Danny Kaye had a terrific arrangement... I refused to play it. I played the rhythm but I changed the voicing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002
